key.name: edge-tolerance
key.description: The distance in pixels from the screen edge that triggers the warp.

key.name: pressure-threshold-ms
key.description: The time in milliseconds the cursor must press against the edge before warping.

key.name: is-enabled
key.description: Whether the mouse warp functionality is currently active.

key.name: warp-enabled
key.description: Whether the proportional warp logic is active (can be disabled to isolate debug visuals).

key.name: overlay-enabled
key.description: Show a colored circle following the cursor that changes color per monitor.

key.name: click-flash-enabled
key.description: Flash a dot at the true click position on each button press.

key.name: monitor-config
key.description: JSON mapping monitor index to overlay color and size.

key.name: warp-cooldown-ms
key.description: Cooldown in milliseconds after a warp before another can trigger. Prevents double-warps from a single crossing.

key.name: overlap-remap-enabled
key.description: Whether to proportionally remap the x-coordinate when crossing between monitor rows at overlap zones. Disable to only use dead-zone pressure warping.

key.name: visual-feedback-enabled
key.description: Show a blue glow circle at the warp destination when a warp occurs.

key.name: debug-logging
key.description: Log warp events and diagnostics to the GNOME Shell journal.

key.name: hide-top-bar
key.description: Hide the GNOME top bar (panel). Reclaims screen space and eliminates top-edge hitbox interference with monitor boundary detection.

key.name: poll-rate-ms
key.description: Interval in milliseconds between cursor position polls. Lower = more responsive but higher CPU. Default 8ms (~120Hz).

key.name: row-tolerance
key.description: Maximum Y-offset in pixels for grouping monitors into the same row. Increase if monitors have slight vertical misalignment.
